overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a focused and active presence across three academic/professional social media platforms: LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon. With a combined total of 3,580 followers and 7 recent posts, the profile primarily shares updates on research, publications, grants, and career opportunities within the computational social science and disinformation fields. Overall engagement averages around 1.70% per post relative to the total follower base, with LinkedIn having the largest audience and Bluesky demonstrating the highest average engagement rate per post.

contentThemes.description: Updates on research methodologies, software releases (e.g., CooRnet), and expertise in computational social science and data analysis.
Discussions and research findings related to coordinated inauthentic behavior, disinformation, and information operations on social media.
Announcements of new papers, research grants, conference presentations, and general academic discussions.
Posts related to hiring, postdoctoral positions, and PhD student opportunities within relevant research fields.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's higher engagement rate by consistently sharing research findings, upcoming presentations, and informal calls for collaboration to maximize reach within the academic community.

Cross-promote key announcements (e.g., grant awards, major publications, hiring calls) across all platforms, tailoring the message slightly for each audience (e.g., more formal on LinkedIn, more conversational on Bluesky/Mastodon).

Consider increasing posting frequency on Mastodon or experimenting with different content formats (e.g., asking open-ended questions for discussions) to boost engagement, as its current per-post engagement is lower than other platforms.
Continue to utilize LinkedIn for formal announcements such as grants and hiring, as it reaches the largest professional audience and garners significant engagement for these types of updates.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits the highest average engagement rate (6.14%) per post, demonstrating strong resonance for direct announcements about new publications and research opportunities. It appears to be a highly effective platform for engaging with an active academic audience.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon is used for academic updates and discussions, featuring a smaller follower base (780) and a lower average engagement rate (4.55%) compared to other platforms. It serves as a niche platform for connecting with specific academic peers.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn boasts the largest follower count (1,850) and performs exceptionally well for formal announcements like grant awards and recruitment drives, securing a strong average engagement rate of 4.86%. It is the primary platform for professional networking and major career-related news.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
